title: caladenia carnea var. alba
intro: Caladenia carnea var. alba, also known as the pink hood orchid, is a rare and endemic variety of orchid found in Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine, and is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations and reducing fever, and has been used to treat various ailments including cuts, burns, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Caladenia carnea var. alba include infusions made from the plant's leaves and roots, which can be applied topically or consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
